item.title: CLARITY Act faces Aug. 10 Senate deadline as three disputes - ethics provisions, stablecoin yield rules, and oversight scope - stall a floor vote.
Senate released an updated CLARITY Act draft July 22 adding a provision banning federal officials from issuing digital assets.
MiCA took effect July 1; EU exchanges delisted USDT after Tether did not seek EMT authorization, leaving Circle's USDC as the sole licensed stablecoin.
EU regulators launched a MiCA stablecoin framework revision July 9 after reserve requirements concentrated the European market around a single issuer.
GENIUS Act stablecoin law reached its July 18 one-year mark with OCC, FDIC, NCUA, Treasury, FinCEN, and OFAC under deadline to finalize implementing rules.
SEC scheduled three crypto rulemakings for July targeting broker-dealer capital requirements, customer asset protection, and recordkeeping standards.
California's Digital Financial Assets Law took effect July 1, requiring state licensing for all entities conducting crypto business with California residents.
OKX Europe enabled direct USDT-to-USDC conversion for EU customers as exchanges adapt operations to MiCA stablecoin compliance requirements.
FATF Travel Rule now enacted in 73% of assessed jurisdictions; the EU applies a zero-value threshold for all CASP-to-CASP transfers.
BlackRock's IBIT held 734,762 BTC valued at $48 billion as of July 15; over 80% of US spot Bitcoin ETF assets are held through a single custodian.
H1 2026 crypto hacks totaled $955 million across 344 incidents; wallet compromises surpassed smart contract exploits as the leading loss vector.
North Korean threat actors were linked to $643 million - 66% of all crypto stolen globally in H1 2026, per TRM Labs.
Ostium, an RWA perpetuals protocol on Arbitrum, lost $24 million in an oracle manipulation exploit on July 15.
BonkDAO, Bonzo Lend, and Summer.fi suffered combined losses of $35 million in DeFi exploits during the week of July 7.
